main_useful_patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved
uncertainties: Do not construct success_like preference pairs because no high-confidence success_like trajectory exists.
Do not treat partial_progress as success_like; only weak preference pairs are allowed.

forbidden_changes: do not only scale all coefficients
do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not treat noisy partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: explicitly avoid known failures: early_failure, low_progress_survival
preserve useful patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved
gate or remove components: crash_penalty, low_progress_survival_gate, stability_penalty, timeout_penalty, ungated_contact_bonus, ungated_survival_bonus
preserve or strengthen components: approach_progress, leg_bonus, near_stage_stability, progress_delta, terminal_failure_penalty

rollback_decision.reason: Hard rollback is handled by RollbackManager, not Analyzer.

self_evaluation_lesson: Trajectory memory supports preference-guided mutation, but partial progress must remain distinct from success.

previous_selection_used.allowed_decision: Use weak preference pairs to select a candidate for long training with low-confidence warning.

previous_selection_used.reason: Selected valid candidate using weak preference evidence.

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Bootstrap round establishes baseline trajectory coverage; progress-conditioned mutation yields partial progress but no success. Further mutation should aim to increase success-like behavior.
future_guidance: preserve progress_delta if it favors partial_progress over early_failure
target terminal stability to convert partial_progress into success
avoid heavy early_failure penalty that may suppress exploration

archive_update.reason: bootstrap round - selected candidate is first usable reward function

uncertainties: No success-like trajectories exist, so success-level preference cannot be constructed
Low number of low_progress_survival trajectories may indicate reward function already discourages survival hacking

observed_outcome.main_success_signal: approach_progress remains consistent; leg_contact_bonus shows mixed evidence but near_stage_stability is weak

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: stability_penalty penalized partial_progress trajectories, blocking conversion to success
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: restructured stability_penalty into positive stability reward; added leg_contact_bonus, near_stage_stability, and soft_landing_bonus; gated by progress
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: failure_avoidance improved (mean reward on failure trajectories became positive), but partial_progress trajectories dropped from 23 to 1 in candidate evaluation; no success_like trajectories emerged

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Replacing stability_penalty with positive stability reward improved failure_reward but may have been too restrictive; near_stage_stability component must be strengthened to favor partial_progress over failures and support progress toward success.
future_guidance: preserve approach_progress and leg_contact_bonus as they show some preference consistency
strengthen near_stage_stability alignment to encourage conversion from partial_progress to success-like behavior
ensure positive stability reward does not overly reduce partial_progress counts
do not treat partial_progress as success_like
avoid adding global survival bonus without progress gating

archive_update.reason: Selected candidate improved training return (from -4.63 to -3.58) and is used as next parent; no rollback triggered

uncertainties: The large decrease in partial_progress trajectories may indicate the new reward is too strict, but the training data may also differ from the memory used in coverage report
No success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preferences cannot be constructed
Weak evidence for near_stage_stability component consistency (0.13); conclusions about its effectiveness are preliminary

observed_outcome.main_success_signal: all 50 training trajectories became partial_progress; training return improved from -3.58 to -0.31

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: Previous component_recomposition reduced partial_progress from 23 to 1, possibly due to over-restrictive near_stage_stability component.
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: Further component_recomposition aiming to strengthen near_stage_stability while preserving partial_progress and avoiding survival hacking.
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: Partial_progress count recovered to 50; no success-like trajectories yet.

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Strengthening near_terminal stability through component_recomposition can fully recover partial_progress, but converting partial_progress into success requires additional mechanisms beyond current formulation.
future_guidance: preserve current ability to generate partial_progress trajectories
target success-like behavior by introducing explicit success conditions or terminal rewards
avoid over-restrictive stability that reduces partial_progress
do not construct success preference pairs yet; margin still insufficient

archive_update.reason: Selected candidate achieved all partial_progress trajectories and best training return so far; used as next parent.

uncertainties: No success-like trajectories exist, so success-level preferences cannot be constructed.
The jump from 1 to 50 partial_progress may be influenced by training seed or environment variability.
Label-margin between partial_progress and low_progress_survival is still weak; progress preference construction remains invalid.

observed_outcome.main_success_signal: selected candidate produces only partial_progress trajectories, eliminating early failures and low_progress_survival in its own evaluation

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: no success_like trajectories; safe_landing_bonus never fires due to overly strict condition
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: restructured safe_landing_bonus to provide partial credit for sub-conditions and added intermediate rewards for leg contact and stability near pad
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: candidate generates 50 partial_progress trajectories but still no success_like achieved; safe_landing_bonus remains insufficient to trigger final terminal condition

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Local repair that provides intermediate rewards for progress toward terminal condition can eliminate early failures and produce consistent partial progress, but bridging the gap to success_like requires aligning reward with the exact terminal state rather than intermediate approximations; pure partial-progress dominance does not imply success readiness.
future_guidance: preserve approach_progress component as it drives partial_progress
avoid treating partial_progress as success_like in preference construction
strengthen terminal condition reward with progressive relaxation or shaping to encourage the final landing step
consider using curriculum learning or gradual tightening of safe_landing condition to guide policy from partial_progress to success_like
